question.text: A trader buys 10 lots of USDINR futures at 83.00. Next day DSP is 83.20, then 82.90, then at expiry FSP is 83.50. What is the total P&L?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Total P&L = (FSP - Entry) × 1000 × 10 = (83.50 - 83.00) × 10,000 = Rs. 5,000 profit. The intermediate MTM settlements (Day 1: +Rs. 2,000, Day 2: -Rs. 3,000, Expiry: +Rs. 6,000) net to the same total. **The Trap:** Total P&L = Final price minus entry price. Daily MTM is just intermediate cash flow — the total is the same. **Cross-Exam Context:** Multi-day P&L tracking is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** While total P&L is the same, daily MTM affects cash flow requirements. The Day 2 loss of Rs. 3,000 would require margin top-up.
